commit.message:
fix: MRP 원재료 소요량 과다 산출 근본 수정 — forceLeafPns 적용

근본 원인: BOM이 사출/도장 부품 하위의 원재료(RAB0551 ABS, RLX0461 LEXAN 등)까지
전개하여 leaf.totalRequired를 KG로 오인 → 273톤 ABS, 191톤 LEXAN 등 비현실적 수량

수정:
- expandBomToLeaves: forceLeafPns 파라미터 실제 작동하도록 변경
  (기존: 자식 있으면 무시 → 수정: 강제 리프로 처리)
- MRP: 사출/도장 부품(refInfo processType)을 forceLeafPns에 등록
  → BOM 전개 시 사출/도장 부품에서 멈추고, Path 3/4가 netWeight·paintQty로 정확한 KG 산출
  → 하위 원재료 코드(RAB0551 등)가 별도 리프로 중복 산출되지 않음

예상: RAB0551 273,245EA → Path 3 정확한 KG, RLX0461 191,286KG → 첨가제 보정 적용

commit.message:
fix: 07-02 perf regression + add segment-level cohort analysis (09-25/26)

07-01: Limit Other Banks to big_nationals/digital_banks/fed_district
with only PAYMENT/TRANSFER suffixes (was generating 500+ patterns from
all competitor categories × 5 suffixes, choking str.contains regex).

07-02: Batch regex matching in groups of 80 to prevent massive
alternation patterns from hanging.

09-25: New segment cohort data cell -- classifies each mailed account
into challenge segment (NU, TH-10, TH-15, TH-20, TH-25) and computes
monthly spend from -6 to +36 months relative to mail date. Compares
responders vs non-responders within each segment.

09-26: Multi-panel spend trajectory chart + DID lift bar chart by
segment and time window (3mo, 6mo, 12mo, 24mo, 36mo post).

fix: detect claude-in-chrome at agent runtime instead of config file

claude-in-chrome is injected by a Chrome extension at runtime and never
appears in ~/.claude.json mcpServers. The preflight script could never
detect it, always reporting Grok as "not ready" for chrome users.

- SKILL.md: agent checks for mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools first,
  skips shell preflight entirely if present (self-evident to agent)
- grok_setup.sh: add --backend override flag as escape hatch for
  callers that know their backend (e.g. other scripts/integrations)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix page stability: prevent session state loss during navigation

Root cause: clear_cache() called st.cache_data.clear() (nuclear option)
every time user switched SKUs, destroying ALL page caches and causing
subsequent page loads to re-fetch from DB. Combined with no connection
timeout, this caused pages to hang indefinitely, dropping the WebSocket
and losing all session state.

Fixes:
- Remove clear_cache() from SKU change in pricing_tool_main.py
- Replace st.cache_data.clear() with targeted per-function .clear()
- Add try/except to _load_products(), _load_products_from_db(),
  load_table() to prevent unhandled DB errors from crashing pages
- Add Connection Timeout=15s and pool_timeout=10s to DB engine
- Call clear_cache() only after CSV sync (where it's actually needed)

commit.message:
feat: remove d<=1024 ceiling from all CUDA memory kernels (#173)

* feat: remove d<=1024 ceiling from all CUDA memory kernels

Convert all `if (tid < d)` patterns to strided `for` loops across 10
kernel files, enabling d=2048 for the 700M-1B HOPE model on H100.
Gate backward removed assert(D<=512), now supports D up to 512 with
min(2*D,1024) block sizing. C wrapper guards changed from hard d>1024
to shared-memory-based limit (d<=5120).

Build system forces optimized CUDA compilation in debug profile to
prevent nvcc -G register inflation from causing launch failures.

10 new d=2048 validation tests confirm numerical match against Rust
reference. All 1118 existing tests pass unchanged (regression safe).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit review — smem guards, serial tests, spec source

- Backward kernel guards: validate against actual smem_bytes (includes
  block_size + 3*d terms) instead of simplified 8*d formula
- Checkpoint forward guards: use 2*d (actual allocation) not 8*d
- Tests: add #[serial(cuda)] to all 10 d=2048 tests per codebase convention
- Spec: expand Source field with CUDA Programming Guide references

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: round 2 review — ckpt guard formulas, spec test table accuracy

- dgd_forward_ckpt: restore 8*d guard (ckpt uses Ampere path, not 2*d)
- hebbian_forward_ckpt: smem=0, guard needs only d>0
- Spec table: distinguish full numerical match (delta) from non-zero
  gradient sanity checks (hebbian/titans/dgd backward)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: round 3 review — ckpt overflow guard, spec smem table per-kernel

- hebbian_forward_ckpt: add d<=46340 upper bound (prevents dd=d*d int
  overflow; ckpt uses smem=0 so no smem-based limit applies)
- Spec table: split ckpt row by kernel (delta/titans: 2*d, dgd: 8*d,
  hebbian: 0) to accurately reflect per-kernel allocations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix review Q&A mismatch after question splits

When master has more questions than clone (e.g. Q35 split into
Q35ab + Q35c), the old orderIndex fallback shifted all subsequent
questions off by one.

Now the mark GET API uses master's question list as the base and
maps marking data from clone by questionNum. This ensures the
review always shows the correct answer for each question regardless
of structural differences between master and clone.

commit.message:
Add method1.3: single graph with 100 reduction kernel boundaries (#17)

Method 1.3 uses torch.compile with a single graph (no graph breaks) but
forces ~100 kernel boundaries via sum() reductions. This isolates the
cost of Inductor's multi-kernel dispatch from Dynamo's per-subgraph
overhead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

add empty-parent stale payload_expected_withdrawals test

When a block processes with FULL parent, payload_expected_withdrawals is
populated. If the payload is then missed (envelope never arrives) and the
next block processes with EMPTY parent, process_withdrawals_gloas returns
early WITHOUT clearing payload_expected_withdrawals. The stale value
persists. This tests the missed-payload withdrawal scenario described in
consensus-specs#4962.
